title: hierochloe redolens var. fraseri
intro: Hierochloe redolens var. fraseri, also known as sweet grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly by the indigenous peoples of North America, who valued it for its medicinal and spiritual properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Sweet grass can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or smudge stick to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being, and is also used in aromatherapy to calm the mind and soothe the body.
